spec_match.rationale: The demo now applies explicit background-image, background-origin, and background-clip layer lists for the old padding-box/border-box stack and the new border-area/padding-box stack; DevTools MCP verification is still blocked in this session.

interactive.rationale: The controls are wired to update both cards and generated CSS snippets, but the required chrome-devtools-mcp click-through verification was unavailable.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature and concept blurbs describe a side-by-side comparison of the old two-layer hack and the new border-area composition, which is exactly what the repaired code renders.

edge_cases.rationale: The unsupported-browser warning remains in place and the conformance suite now covers composed layer lists, but the fallback was not exercised through DevTools MCP.

design_system.rationale: Uses the correct theme CSS variables, high-contrast code boxes, and clean grid placements.

openQuestions.title: Run DevTools MCP verification for multi-layer composition
openQuestions.detail: chrome-devtools-mcp was not exposed in this repair session. The page should be reopened in Chrome or Chrome Canary, every range/select control should be exercised, #cardOld and #cardNew inline styles should be inspected for populated background-image/background-origin/background-clip layer data, and the /v150/css-background-clip-border-area/conformance/ route should be checked.

summary: Static repair changed the comparison away from ambiguous background shorthands to explicit per-layer longhands so the old card visibly uses the padding-box/border-box hack and the new card exposes inspectable border-area/padding-box composition data. Browser verification remains blocked until chrome-devtools-mcp is available.
